Fern Polrrrcer_ pRAcrrcps CoMMrssroN
428 J Str.eet o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA g5gl4_Z3lg
(916) 322_5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 1,2008
Susan Orman
Monterey County Elections
Candidate Services
1370 B South Main Street
Salinas, CA 93901
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-0g_110
Dear Ms. Orman
This letter is inresponse to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the ;Act,,;.1 "
QUESTION
May the committees involved in an Augu st26,200g, mail ballot election combine
the semi-annual sratement with the first p..-"[-";;;.i"i.-"", due on Jury 17,200g?
CONCLUSION
ln connection with the August 26,2oo8,mail ballot election, committees may use
a filing schedule that combines thJ semi-annual campaign statement with the first pre-
election statement. The combined statement will be iLe-on July 17, 200g, covering the
period January l, ZO0g, through July 12,200g.
rThe PoliticalRefbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections g1000 through 91014. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government code, unless otherwise inal.ut"a. The regulati.ns of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in sections.ral ry ,trrrrei r g997 .f ritre 2 of the Carifornia
Code of Regttlations' All regulatory r.f"r"n...r-rre to Title 2, Divisioii of the califtrrnia Code,f
Regulations, unless otherwise inOicateO.


File No. A-08-110
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions received
and expenditures made in connection with that election. These-pre-election 
"ur,puig,
statements are general!_ri]ea 40 days before the election and again 12 days u"ror" tr,.
election' (Section 84200.8') Semi-annual statements are filed January 3 1 and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-"election statements. (Section g4205.)
In this case, if the semi-annual and first pre-eleition statements are not combined, the
semi-annual statement, covering the period through lr"" :0, 200g, will be filed on
July 31, 2008, which is after the first pre-election statement is due. Therefore,
committees involved in the August 26,zoo8,mail ballot election may combine the two
filings' A campaign statement iiting schedule that outlines the due dates for filing
statements in connection with the election is enclosed for your convenience.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: TaraL. Stock
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
TSjgl
